pi_items.source: Task instruction explicitly requests saving to /home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/merchant_plant_results.json and export_result.sh checks for this exact path.
Independent PySAM simulation of a 100MW DC tracking system in Phoenix yields ~211.9 GWh, which fits precisely within the claimed 180-280 GWh boundary.
Independent PySAM simulation of a 100MW DC tracking system in Phoenix yields ~211.9 GWh, which fits precisely within the claimed 180-280 GWh boundary.
Independent PySAM simulation yields a DC capacity factor of ~24.2%, fitting within the claimed 20-32% bounds.

Independent PySAM simulation mapping the synthetic ERCOT LMP CSV array to the Merchant Plant revenue model yields ~$4.9M, fitting within the claimed 1M-20M bounds.
Independent PySAM simulation mapping the synthetic ERCOT LMP CSV array to the Merchant Plant revenue model yields ~$4.9M, fitting within the claimed 1M-20M bounds.

privileged_info_summary: I confirmed that the task operates on a synthetically generated ERCOT LMP pricing dataset built at runtime by setup_task.sh. By independently modeling the 100 MW DC tracking PV system via PySAM using Phoenix TMY weather data, I verified the physical and financial outputs: annual energy (~211.9 GWh), DC capacity factor (~24.2%), and Year 1 energy revenue (~$4.9M) all fall well within the bounded metadata ranges. I also discovered a critical defect: 'verifier.py' is entirely missing from the environment, making the task programmatically unscorable because the success specification points to a non-existent script.
